tex:
**[2024 Oct/Nov, 0580/32, Question 1]**

(a) Onions cost \$1.85 per kilogram and potatoes cost \$2.34 per kilogram. \\
Sophie buys 2.4~kg of onions and 4.5~kg of potatoes. \\
Work out how much change she receives from \$20.

(b) Sophie gets on a bus at 10 47 and she gets off the bus 36 minutes later. \\
Work out the time she gets off the bus.

(c) Sophie uses two-thirds of a litre of milk each day. \\
Milk is sold in 2-litre bottles. \\
Show that she needs at least 3 bottles to have enough milk for 7 days.

(d) One month, Helen sells phones and computers in the ratio
\[ \text{number of phones} : \text{number of computers} = 3 : 5 \]
She sells 40 computers. \\
Work out how many phones she sells.

(e) In 2022 Helen sells 520 phones. \\
In 2023 she sells 35\% more phones than in 2022. \\
Calculate the number of phones she sells in 2023.

(f) A television costs \$840 in the USA. \\
The same television costs 3549 ringgits in Malaysia. \\
The exchange rate is \$1 = 4.2 ringgits. \\
In which country is the television cheaper and by how many dollars?

**[2024 Oct/Nov, 0580/32, Question 2]**

(a) \[ 3.142 \quad 87 \quad 14 \quad 41 \quad 56 \quad 117 \quad 121 \]

From this list, write down
(i) (b) an odd number

    

(c) a factor of 28

    

(d) a square number

    

(e) a prime number

    

(f) a multiple of 9.

    

(g) Write down the value of $5^0$.

(h) Write 60 as a product of its prime factors.

(i) Calculate the value of $\sqrt[3]{157\,464}$.

(j) By rounding each number in the calculation correct to 1 significant figure, find an estimate for the value of
\[ \frac{67.8 \times 2.38}{4.803 + 29.87} \]
You must show all your working.

(k) (i) (l) \[ 9.78 \times 10^8 \quad 2.04 \times 10^9 \]
    Which of these two numbers is larger? \\
    Give a reason for your answer.

    

(m) Calculate $1.732 \times 10^3 \div 5.73 \times 10^{-1}$. \\
    Give your answer in standard form.

    

(n) Two cars go round a track. \\
One car completes each lap of the track in 96 seconds. \\
The other car completes each lap in 120 seconds. \\
Both cars start a lap together at 08 37. \\
Find the next time when both cars start a lap together.

**[2024 Oct/Nov, 0580/32, Question 3]**

(a) A sequence of shapes is made with squares and triangles.

(i) (b) On the grid, draw Shape 3.


(c) Find the number of triangles in Shape 5.

    

(d) These are the first four terms of a sequence.
\[ 32 \quad 25 \quad 18 \quad 11 \]
(i) (e) Find the next two terms.

    

(f) Write down the term to term rule for this sequence.

    

(g) (i) (h) \[ 5, \quad 8, \quad 11, \quad 14, \quad \dots \]
    Find the $n$th term of this sequence.

    

(i) \[ 1, \quad 8, \quad 27, \quad 64, \quad \dots \]
    Find the $n$th term of this sequence.

**[2024 Oct/Nov, 0580/32, Question 4]**

(a) Calculate the volume of a cylinder with radius 7.8~cm and height 15~cm.

(b) A cube has a volume of 3375~cm$^3$. \\
Calculate the surface area of this cube.

(c) \[ \text{Area } A = 37\,000\text{ cm}^2 \quad \text{Area } B = 5.4\text{ m}^2 \]
Which of these two areas is the larger? \\
You must show all your working.

(d) The diagram shows a right-angled triangle $ABC$.

Calculate angle $ACB$.

(e) The diagram shows a rectangle $DEFG$.

$DE = 12$~cm and $DF = 31.2$~cm. \\
Calculate the area of the rectangle $DEFG$.

**[2024 Oct/Nov, 0580/32, Question 5]**

(a) 24 students do a puzzle. \\
The table shows the time, $t$ minutes, each student takes to complete the puzzle.

\begin{tabular}{|l|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
Time ($t$ minutes) & 2 & 3 & 4 & 5 & 6 & 7 \\ \hline
Frequency          & 2 & 4 & 8 & 7 & 12 & 7 \\ \hline
\end{tabular}
(i) (b) Find the mode.

    

(c) Calculate the mean.

    

(d) In another puzzle each student gets a score. \\
These are the scores for 12 students.
\[ 17 \quad 21 \quad 24 \quad 32 \quad 27 \quad 11 \]
\[ 26 \quad 18 \quad 10 \quad 29 \quad 14 \quad 24 \]

(i) (e) Complete the stem-and-leaf diagram for these scores.

    

(f) Find the median.

    

(g) A different puzzle has three outcomes: win, draw or lose. \\
The table shows the outcomes for 30 students.

\begin{tabular}{|l|c|}
\hline
Outcome & Frequency \\ \hline
Win     & 9         \\ \hline
Draw    & 14        \\ \hline
Lose    & 7         \\ \hline
\end{tabular}
Complete the pie chart to show this information.

tex:
**[2024 Oct/Nov, 0580/32, Question 6]**

(a) The diagram shows a cuboid.

On the 1~cm$^2$ grid, complete a net of this cuboid. \\
One face has been drawn for you.

(b) Three regular pentagons meet at point $A$.

Work out the value of $x$.

(c) The diagram shows two parallel lines and two straight lines.

(i) (d) Find the value of $x$. \\
    Give a geometrical reason for your answer.

    

(e) Find the value of $y$.

**[2024 Oct/Nov, 0580/32, Question 7]**

(a) $\mathscr{E} = \{ \text{students in a group} \}$ \\
$M = \{ \text{students who pass the mathematics test} \}$ \\
$S = \{ \text{students who pass the science test} \}$ \\
142 students are in the group. \\
105 students pass the mathematics test. \\
82 students pass the mathematics test and pass the science test. \\
17 students do not pass the mathematics test and do not pass the science test.

(i) (b) Complete the Venn diagram.

    

(c) Find $n(M \cup S)$.

    

(d) One of these students is picked at random. \\
    Find the probability that this student passes the science test but does not pass the mathematics test.

    

(e) Use set notation to describe the shaded region.

(f) In a town, the number of students, $n$, who take the science test is 10\,600, correct to the nearest hundred. \\
Complete this statement about the value of $n$.

(g) The table shows the number of students in another town who took the science test in 2022 and 2023.

\begin{tabular}{|l|c|c|}
\hline
Year               & 2022   & 2023   \\ \hline
Number of students & 15\,800 & 17\,064 \\ \hline
\end{tabular}
Calculate the percentage increase in the number of students from 2022 to 2023.

(h) The number of students who took the mathematics test in 2022 is 18\,400. \\
The ratio $\text{number of students who passed} : \text{number of students who did not pass}$ is $4 : 1$. \\
Work out the number of students who passed.

**[2024 Oct/Nov, 0580/32, Question 8]**

(a) 
(i) (b) Find the equation of line $L$ in the form $y = mx + c$.

    

(c) \begin{subsubparts}

(d) Complete the table of values for $y = 8 - 2x$.

        \begin{tabular}{|l|c|c|c|}
        \hline
        $x$ & 0 & 2 & 4 \\ \hline
        $y$ &   & 4 &   \\ \hline
        \end{tabular}


(e) On the grid, draw the graph of $y = 8 - 2x$ for $0 \le x \le 4$.

    \end{subsubparts}

(f) Find the coordinates of the point where line $L$ intersects the graph of $y = 8 - 2x$.

    

(g) (i) (h) Complete the table of values for $y = x^2 - 4x - 4$.

    \begin{tabular}{|l|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
    \hline
    $x$ & $-2$ & $-1$ & 0  & 1  & 2  & 3  & 4  & 5  & 6 \\ \hline
    $y$ & 8    & 1    & $-4$ & $-7$ & $-8$ &    & $-4$ & 1  & 8 \\ \hline
    \end{tabular}


(i) On the grid, draw the graph of $y = x^2 - 4x - 4$ for $-2 \le x \le 6$.

    

(j) Write down the equation of the line of symmetry of the graph.

    

(k) Use your graph to solve the equation $x^2 - 4x - 4 = 0$.

tex:
**[2024 Oct/Nov, 0580/32, Question 9]**

(a) On the grid, translate triangle $T$ by the vector $\begin{pmatrix} -5 \\ -4 \end{pmatrix}$.

(b) Describe fully the single transformation that maps triangle $T$ onto triangle $A$.

(c) Describe fully the single transformation that maps triangle $T$ onto triangle $B$.
